summary: Kendra Wallace from a retail company contacted Aegis Cloud Security support regarding severely degraded backup performance, with nightly jobs taking 4-6 hours instead of the usual 90 minutes over the past three weeks. David Kim identified a likely root cause: Kendra's environment is running backup agent version 3.4.1, which has a known change block tracking issue that causes over-scanning, while a fix was released in version 3.6.0 in late January. David acknowledged a process gap in that Kendra was not notified about the update. The resolution plan includes upgrading to 3.6.0 and a follow-up call on Monday, with escalation to senior engineering promised if the issue persists.
actionItems: David Kim: Send upgrade instructions to Kendra's inbox within 30 minutes of the call
Kendra Wallace: Schedule agent upgrade to version 3.6.0 tonight or tomorrow night outside the backup window
David Kim: Follow up Monday morning to confirm backup performance improvement and keep the case open
David Kim: Investigate why Kendra's account was not notified about the known issue in version 3.4.x and escalate to senior engineering and account manager if unresolved

keyMoments.text: Kendra describes nightly backup jobs taking 4-6 hours, up from under 90 minutes, lasting three weeks with real operational impact on her retail business
keyMoments.speaker: Kendra Wallace

keyMoments.text: David identifies throughput has dropped from 400-500 MB/s to 120-140 MB/s and flags that Kendra is running an outdated agent version 3.4.1 with a known over-scanning bug
keyMoments.speaker: David Kim

keyMoments.text: Kendra expresses frustration that she was not notified about the known issue and the fix already available in version 3.6.0
keyMoments.speaker: Kendra Wallace

keyMoments.text: David commits to sending upgrade instructions within 30 minutes, following up Monday morning, and escalating to senior engineering if the issue is unresolved
keyMoments.speaker: David Kim
